title: Pain Management, Vasopressors, Inotropes & Antiarrhythmic Agents Quiz

questions.text: What are examples of non-pharmacologic pain relief?

answers.text: Re-positioning in bed and stabilization of fracture

questions.text: What stress responses can pain cause?

answers.text: Tachycardia, elevated myocardial oxygen consumption, pulmonary dysfunction, hypercoagulability (thrombophilia)

questions.text: Analgesia provides absence of sensibility to pain without what?
How does analgesia differ from anesthesia?

answers.text: Analgesia results in complete loss of physical sensation
Anesthesia results in a complete loss of physical sensation

questions.text: NSAIDs are the most common prescribed and what type of analgesic?
What is the indication for NSAIDs?
What is a key limitation of Tylenol (Acetaminophen) compared to NSAIDs?
What are the adverse actions of opioids/narcotics?
Which opioid has lower respiratory depression?
What routes of administration are used for opioids/narcotics?
What is the indication for sedation?

answers.text: Treat anxiety and tension; must be used if delivering paralytic

questions.text: Etomidate (Amidate) is specifically used for what?

answers.text: Rapid sequence induction, conscious sedation, TBI patients

questions.text: What are the indications for benzodiazepines?

answers.text: Anxiety, panic attacks, insomnia and seizures

questions.text: Which benzodiazepine is the most commonly used?
Propofol (Diprivan) is known as what nickname due to its effects?
What are the effects of Propofol (Diprivan)?

answers.text: Decreased LOC and lack of memory for events
Increased heart rate and blood pressure

questions.text: What is the indication for Adenosine?

answers.text: SVT that does not improve with vagal maneuvers (carotid massage, Valsalva)

questions.text: What is the action of Adenosine?

answers.text: Decreases AV node conduction; slows impulse transmission

questions.text: What are the indications for Amiodarone?

answers.text: A Fib, VFib, and ventricular tachyarrhythmias

questions.text: What is the action of Amiodarone?
What are the indications for Atropine Sulfate?

answers.text: Sinus bradycardia; asystole; PEA; organophosphate poisoning

questions.text: What is the action of Atropine Sulfate?

answers.text: Increase HR, and increase force of atrial contractions

questions.text: Dopamine is indicated for hypotension with signs and symptoms of shock and as a second-line drug for what?
What is the action of Dopamine?

answers.text: Increases myocardial contractility, increases cardiac output, cerebral vasodilation, and peripheral arterial and venous vasoconstriction

questions.text: Epinephrine is the 1st line drug therapy for what condition?
What is the action of Epinephrine?

answers.text: Decreases AV node conduction and slows impulse transmission
Stimulates adrenergic receptors; produces vasoconstriction, increases blood pressure, heart rate, and improves perfusion to the brain and heart
Prolongs conduction time caused by hypomagnesemia

questions.text: What is the correct treatment sequence for anaphylaxis?

answers.text: Epinephrine, antihistamines injection, corticosteroids injection, fluids, Trendelenburg

questions.text: Lidocaine is an alternative antiarrhythmic to what drug in cardiac arrest from VF/VT?
What is the action of Lidocaine?

answers.text: Increases electrical stimulation threshold; depresses ventricular electrical activity

questions.text: What is Magnesium Sulfate indicated for?

answers.text: Management of Torsades de Pointe or hypomagnesemia

questions.text: What are the indications for Norepinephrine (Levophed)?

answers.text: Vasogenic shock, septic shock, neurogenic shock

questions.text: What is the action of Norepinephrine?

answers.text: Alpha-adrenergic stimulation; vasoconstriction — increases blood pressure; increases blood flow

questions.text: What is the indication for Oxygen as a cardiac medication?

answers.text: Decreased PaO2; provides all cellular energy and support for myocardial contraction

questions.text: What does Oxygen prevent at the cellular level?
Sedation must be used if delivering what type of agent?
